header.disclaimer: Marketing communication. Please refer to the prospectus and Key Investor Information Document before making any final investment decisions.

metric-value.alt: High yield yield to maturity: 6.79 percent

section.section-description: Understanding if this fund fits your investment needs

profile-item.profile-value: Diversified US equities, HY bonds & convertibles

section.section-description: Monthly insights from the investment team

commentary-block.commentary-paragraph: Market environment: February saw a modest pullback in US equities as investors digested mixed economic signals. The S&P 500 retreated amid persistent inflation readings and trade policy uncertainty. HY spreads widened slightly but remained near historical tights, reflecting resilient corporate fundamentals.

Performance drivers: The fund returned −0.62%. The equity sleeve was the primary detractor as technology holdings (22.8%) faced profit-taking. The HY allocation provided a cushion through steady coupon income, and select convertible positions in healthcare and industrials contributed positively through favorable conversion dynamics.

Positioning & outlook: We maintain our balanced three-sleeve approach (~1/3 each). We see the current yield environment as attractive for income generation. We have modestly increased duration in the HY sleeve to lock in yields and remain constructive on US corporate credit quality.

section.section-description: Net of fees, in USD. Past performance does not predict future returns.

chart-placeholder.alt: Growth of USD 100 invested from Feb 2016 to Feb 2026. Fund grew from 100 to approximately 230, with a significant drawdown in 2022 (−19.71%) followed by strong recovery. Peer median shown as dashed line.

section.callout: What drove returns? Over the past 12 months, the fund's +11.03% return outperformed the peer group median by ~2.5 percentage points. Key contributors were strong equity selection in technology and industrials, combined with above-average carry from the high-yield sleeve. The balanced three-asset-class structure continued to deliver smoother returns versus pure equity or fixed income strategies.

section.section-description: USD Moderate Allocation category — indicative peer data
section.callout: Peer positioning The fund ranks in the top quartile of its Morningstar category over 1 and 3 years, with higher absolute returns partly offset by slightly above-median volatility reflecting its growth-oriented equity tilt.

section.section-description: Asset allocation, sector exposure, and top holdings as of 28 February 2026

allocation-data.alt: Asset allocation: Equity 31.66%, HY Bonds 30.90%, Convertibles 30.85%, Cash 6.59%

subsection.callout: Why this balanced structure? The roughly equal three-way split provides diversification: equities offer growth potential, high-yield bonds generate income, and convertibles provide asymmetric return profiles with equity upside and bond-like downside protection.

subsection.table-footnote: Top 10 = 14.95% of portfolio. For illustrative purposes only.

subsection.callout: Credit positioning The HY portfolio is primarily invested in BB and B-rated credits, balancing yield pickup with credit quality. The effective duration of 3.30 years keeps interest rate sensitivity moderate.

page.label: Risk Profile, Scenarios, Opportunities and Risks

risk-indicator.alt: Risk indicator: level 4 of 7. Lower risk on the left, higher risk on the right.

section.risk-note: Assumes recommended holding period. Even category 1 is not risk-free.
section.callout: What this means for you In a worst case, the fund historically experienced a maximum decline of 21.64%, taking 23 months to recover. The 3-year Sharpe ratio of 0.11 reflects the challenging 2022 environment, while the 5-year ratio of 0.93 better represents risk-adjusted returns over a full market cycle.

section.section-description: Illustrative outcomes based on historical patterns. Not forecasts.
section.table-footnote: For illustrative purposes only. Past performance does not predict future returns.

section.section-description: Key factors to consider before investing

opportunities.list-item: Attractive potential returns combining equities, HY bonds, and convertible bonds
Access to the US capital market — deepest and most liquid globally
Broad diversification across hundreds of securities reduces single-name risk
Additional return potential from active security analysis and tactical allocation
Currency gains possible for non-USD investors in unhedged share classes

risks.list-item: All three asset classes may be subject to volatility; fund price may fluctuate significantly
US market underperformance could disproportionately impact the fund
HY bonds carry higher credit risk; defaults could impact returns
Success of active management and security selection is not guaranteed
Currency losses possible in unhedged share classes for non-USD investors

section.section-description: How environmental, social, and governance factors are considered
section.esg-body: While this fund does not have sustainable investment as its objective, ESG factors are integrated into the investment analysis process. The investment team considers material ESG risks as part of fundamental research across all three asset sleeves. AllianzGI applies firm-wide exclusion policies covering controversial weapons and other areas.
section.esg-link: For detailed information, please refer to the fund's pre-contractual disclosures and the AllianzGI Sustainability and Stewardship Report at allianzgi.com.

section.section-description: Managed by Voya Investment Management LLC
Key facts, share class data, and fee details

cta-block.cta-body: Access the full prospectus, KID, and performance data on our website.

footnote-list.footnote: Performance calculated based on NAV per unit/share (front-end load deducted in first year), assuming distributions reinvested. Calculation uses BVI method. Past performance does not predict future returns. Source: IDS GmbH (unless otherwise indicated). All performance data prior to share class launch (01/10/2013) refers to Allianz Income and Growth - AT - USD (ISIN LU0689472784).
For guidance only and not indicative of future allocation.
The fund can invest in other funds (target funds).
Securities mentioned are for illustrative purposes only and do not constitute a recommendation to buy or sell.
The Risk Indicator shows how likely it is that the product will lose money because of market movements or inability to pay. Even the lowest category does not mean risk-free.
Sales charges: up to 100% of front-end load may be collected by distributor. The all-in fee includes management, administration and distribution fees.
Yield to maturity is the weighted average of YTM of bonds held. It is a portfolio characteristic and does not reflect actual fund income. Expenses are not deducted.
Yield to worst represents the lowest potential yield (excluding default). Calculation before currency hedging. Not suitable as indicator of future performance.

section.morningstar-attribution: Morningstar Rating: © Morningstar, Inc. All rights reserved. Rating as of 31/01/2026. Top 10%: 5 stars; next 22.5%: 4 stars; middle 35%: 3 stars; next 22.5%: 2 stars; bottom 10%: 1 star.
Morningstar Style Box: © Morningstar, Inc. This fund appeared as Large Blend at 31/12/2025.

section.legal-paragraph: Investing involves risk. The value of an investment and the income from it may fall as well as rise and investors might not get back the full amount invested. Investing in fixed income instruments may expose investors to various risks, including but not limited to creditworthiness, interest rate, liquidity and restricted flexibility risks. Changes to the economic environment and market conditions may affect these risks, resulting in an adverse effect to the value of the investment.

During periods of rising nominal interest rates, the values of fixed income instruments are generally expected to decline. Conversely, during periods of declining interest rates, values are generally expected to rise. Liquidity risk may possibly delay or prevent account withdrawals or redemptions.

Allianz Income and Growth is a sub-fund of Allianz Global Investors Fund SICAV, an open-ended investment company with variable share capital organised under the laws of Luxembourg. The value of units/shares may be subject to increased volatility. Past performance does not predict future returns.

If the currency in which the past performance is displayed differs from the currency of the country in which the investor resides, then the investor should be aware that due to exchange rate fluctuations the performance shown may be higher or lower if converted into the investor's local currency. This is for information only and not to be construed as a solicitation or an invitation to make an offer, to conclude a contract, or to buy or sell any securities.
section.legal-heading: For investors in the United Kingdom:
section.legal-paragraph: This Fund is authorised overseas but not in the United Kingdom. Domiciled in Luxembourg, authorised by the CSSF. Recognised in the UK under the Overseas Funds Regime. Managed by Allianz Global Investors GmbH (authorised by BaFin). The Financial Ombudsman Service is unlikely to consider complaints related to the scheme. Investors can submit complaints to the Facilities Agent (Allianz Global Investors UK Limited).

This is a marketing communication issued by Allianz Global Investors GmbH and approved by Allianz Global Investors UK Limited, 199 Bishopsgate, London, EC2M 3TY, allianzglobalinvestors.co.uk. Allianz Global Investors UK Limited (FRN 959195) is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (fca.org.uk).
The Summary of Investor Rights is available in English, French, German, Italian and Spanish at regulatory.allianzgi.com/en/investors-rights.
